events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes spool erection & fit-up work that is rough-complete, with a defect: flange bolt-hole rotation wrong (two-holing missed). This is a non-compliant condition per ASME B31.3 and project iso drawings. Two-holing is a standard piping practice where bolt holes straddle the centerline. Missing this means the flange orientation is wrong and needs rework.

Decision: fail (non-compliant defect)
Risk: medium (flange rotation wrong can cause fit-up issues with mating flanges, misalignment, potential leak paths)
Work_stage: rough-complete
Component_conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
Percent_complete: 70 (as stated, until defects corrected)
Value: 0 (no numeric reading asked)
Confidence: 0.9
Findings: flange bolt-hole rotation incorrect, two-holing missed
Actions: re-orient flange to correct bolt-hole rotation per iso drawing, re-fit spool, recheck alignment
References: ASME B31.3, project iso drawings
